kpiRibbon.note: All KPI ribbon values verified correct against computeMunicipalMetrics with default slider settings (enforcement=50, digital=30, correction=ON)

netFiscalImpact.note: 5-card grid and Skopje aggregate per-capita verified correct against NET_FISCAL sums

divergingBarChart.note: Per-capita values computed as (revenueInflow - budgetOutflow) / MKD_PER_EUR / workingAgePop. All 28 municipalities verified. Diverging layout correct: 14 gainers on right (green), 14 losers on left (rose).

issues.description: Hardcoded €400M 'City Budget Surplus' at SkopjeCapitalSection.jsx line 68 is FABRICATED
issues.recommendation: Replace hardcoded €400M with either dynamic computation from NET_FISCAL (€123M) for consistency with the rest of the app, or with the actual realized skopje.gov.mk surplus (€6M). The €400M is wrong by €277M or €394M depending on which source is authoritative.

issues.description: Saraj borough missing from SKOPIE_PROPERTY_TAX dataset (9/10 boroughs covered)
issues.recommendation: Source property tax data for Saraj from CCC Open Data or mark as 'no data'

issues.description: nationalAvgOwnRevenuePC is hardcoded to 500 (EUR) in fiscalCapacity.js line 21
issues.impact: Estimated capacity values are relative, not absolute. The capacity gap direction (positive/negative) is still valid, but magnitudes are arbitrary.
issues.recommendation: Compute dynamic nationalAvgOwnRevenuePC from actual NET_FISCAL data: sum of all own revenue / total national population
issues.actualNationalAvg: Would be approximately (total revenueInflow / total pop) = ~€1,580/capita based on NET_FISCAL data

logisticRegressionModel.note: 72.2% accuracy on 18 non-Skopje municipalities vs paper's 82.4% on 74 municipalities. Acceptable — expected due to proxy variable differences and different sample. Struga and Kočani are at the decision boundary (49% and 47%).

gruevskiGaber2010_table1.ourData_note: We use revenueInflow (total Treasury expenditure from basic budget), not 'own revenue' as defined in the paper. These are different concepts — our values are much larger (38K-128K MKD/capita vs paper's 1.3K-6K MKD). The paper uses 'сопствени приходи' (own-source revenue), we use total expenditure from the basic budget which includes VAT grant balancing. Not directly comparable.
gruevskiGaber2010_table1.propertyTaxBenchmark: Paper #03: MK property tax 0.46% GDP vs EU-28 2.5%. €50M gov bailout context.

dataQualityIssues.description: Per-borough Treasury data for Skopje shows extremely large numbers (e.g., Aerodrom €59M revenueInflow) compared to the consolidated City of Skopje budget (€110M total revenue). This suggests double-counting of shared Skopje-level revenues allocated to each borough. The Skopje aggregate in NET_FISCAL (€478M revenue, €355M outflow) is 4x the consolidated city budget. This inflates the apparent Skopje surplus.
dataQualityIssues.recommendation: Document this discrepancy in the MethodologyPanel. Consider using consolidated skopje.gov.mk figures for the Capital Section instead of per-borough NET_FISCAL sums.

dataQualityIssues.description: The fiscal capacity model uses hardcoded nationalAvgOwnRevenuePC=500 which makes all estimated values internally consistent but not accurate in absolute terms.
dataQualityIssues.recommendation: Compute dynamically from NET_FISCAL: total revenueInflow / total national population = ~€1,580/capita. Use this as the benchmark.

dataQualityIssues.description: Saraj borough has no property tax data. The CCC 2017-2019 data totals ~€10M vs skopje.gov.mk 2025 property tax of ~€17M — this is within expected growth range but spans different years.
dataQualityIssues.recommendation: Source Saraj data or mark as 'No data'. Add note that CCC data is 2017-2019, not 2025.
